title: 2x4 Outdoor Side Table
summary: A chunky outdoor side table from four 2x4s and one box of screws — every dimension parameterized, so it resizes to whatever your patio actually needs.
description:
Four 2x4s, fifty screws, no glue, no jig: this is close to the minimum viable table, and it looks better than it has any right to because the design leans into the chunkiness instead of hiding it. The top boards sit INSIDE an apron frame rather than on top of it, so the finished surface reads as a solid slab with a border.

The cut list is really a formula — every dimension is given as 'or desired size minus X': legs are overall height minus 1-1/2", side aprons are depth minus 3", the top infill is width minus 7". Build it as drawn (about 24" x 20" x 18" tall) or resize it into a coffee table or bench-height seat without changing anything about the method.

The joinery is just screws driven smart: wide-spaced, angled in opposing directions where the inner frame meets the legs, which locks the joint against racking far better than parallel screws would. Pick 'red-toned' 2x4s at the store (usually a hem-fir mix that takes stain evenly) and check each board for crown before buying — a table this simple has nowhere for a twisted board to hide.

tools.note: Every cut is a straight crosscut.
Break the top edges well — drinks and forearms live here.

materials.name: 2x4, 8 ft
materials.note: Red-toned boards (hem-fir) stain more evenly than yellow whitewood. Sight each board for crown before buying.

materials.name: Construction screws, #8 x 2-3/4", star drive, exterior
Exterior finish
materials.note: A partial quart does it — or leave it raw and let it grey.

cutList.note: Or 1-1/2" less than your desired overall height.

steps.title: Build the two leg pairs
steps.body: Screw a leg side apron to two legs, flush on the inside faces, two screws per joint. Build two identical assemblies.
materials: Construction screws, #8 x 2-3/4", star drive, exterior

steps.title: Cap the legs with the top frame sides
steps.body: Attach a 24" top board across the top of each leg pair with three screws — one at each end, one in the middle. These two boards are the long sides of the top's border frame.
materials: Construction screws, #8 x 2-3/4", star drive, exterior

steps.title: Build the inner frame
steps.body: Screw the two 18" inside-frame pieces between two of the 17" boards, two screws per joint, forming a rectangle. This frame is what carries the top infill.
materials: Construction screws, #8 x 2-3/4", star drive, exterior

steps.title: Join the inner frame to the legs
steps.body: Set the inner frame between the two leg assemblies and drive four screws per side — spaced wide apart and ANGLED in different directions. The opposing angles are the trick: parallel screws pull out together under racking; opposed ones lock the joint like dovetailed nails.
materials: Construction screws, #8 x 2-3/4", star drive, exterior

steps.title: Fill the top
steps.body:
Lay the remaining 17" boards inside the frame, snug them evenly, and screw each down with one screw per end.

Sand everything, round the top edges well, and finish (or don't — bare framing lumber outdoors just goes silver). Then make the second one, because side tables are like bookends.
materials: Construction screws, #8 x 2-3/4", star drive, exterior

images.alt: Chunky square outdoor side table built entirely from 2x4 lumber
